value.found_quote: Containers provide isolation through Linux namespaces. Each container has its own view of the filesystem, process tree, and network stack, while sharing the host kernel. A security-hardened container configuration might look like this: docker run \ --cap-drop ALL \ --security-opt no-new-privileges \ --security-opt seccomp=/path/to/seccomp-profile.json \ --read-only \ --tmpfs /tmp:rw,noexec,nosuid,size=100m \ --tmpfs /home/agent:rw,noexec,nosuid,size=500m \ --network none \ --memory 2g \ --cpus 2 \ --pids-limit 100 \ --user 1000:1000 \ -v /path/to/code:/workspace:ro \ -v /var/run/proxy.sock:/var/run/proxy.sock:ro \ agent-image
value.rationale: The page explicitly describes running Claude Code or the Agent SDK inside Docker/OCI containers, providing isolation through Linux namespaces, with security-hardened configuration options including seccomp profiles (--security-opt seccomp=...), network policies (--network none), mounted volumes (-v flags), and Linux capability restrictions. The page lists "Containers (Docker)" as one of the supported isolation technologies in a comparison table, and provides detailed configuration guidance for it. All key elements of the claim — Docker/OCI containers, Linux namespaces isolation, security-hardened configuration, network policies, mounted volumes, and seccomp profiles — are substantiated on the page.
